---
title: "Path Modeling"
---

<span class="section-badge advanced">Advanced Topics</span>

::: {.coming-soon}
## 🚧

**This page is under construction.**

Content covering path modeling — and its relationship to SEM — will be added here.
:::

---

## What this page will cover

Path modeling extends multiple regression to systems of equations with multiple dependent variables. It allows you to:

- Estimate **direct** and **indirect** effects simultaneously across a network of variables
- Test theoretical models about causal pathways
- Include **latent variables** (measured variables with error) — the combination of path modeling and latent variables is **structural equation modeling (SEM)**

Common applications in psychology: cross-lagged panel models (measuring variables at two time points to examine reciprocal relationships), growth curve models, and complex mediation models with multiple mediators.

**Relationship to mediation**: a simple mediation model (X → M → Y) is a path model. The [Mediation & Moderation](mediation-moderation.qmd) page covers simple cases using PROCESS. This page will cover more complex path structures.

---

## Useful resources in the meantime

- Kline, R. B. (2023). *Principles and practice of structural equation modeling* (5th ed.). Guilford Press.
- The **lavaan** R package is widely used for SEM: [lavaan.org](https://lavaan.ugent.be)

Start with the [Mediation & Moderation](mediation-moderation.qmd) and [Factor Analysis](factor-analysis.qmd) pages before tackling full path models.
